title: Remote assistance fix (PM2 cluster + frame poll storm)
description:
Remote support screen share was failing on production because PM2 runs multiple Node workers. WebRTC signals and HTTP frame relay data lived in each worker's memory only — the admin often polled a different worker than the customer, so video never connected and the admin hammered /frame ~30 times per second until the browser hit ERR_INSUFFICIENT_RESOURCES and nginx returned 502.

Signals, frames, and control input are now stored in Postgres (shared across all workers). Admin frame polling only runs for dom-relay/http-control modes, uses one in-flight request at a time, and backs off on errors. WebRTC tab share no longer triggers pointless frame polling.

Who is affected: anyone using Remote help / Remote assistance on pipeshare.live and pipeshare.net.
changeLog: Postgres tables for remote WebRTC signals, viewport frames, and control input.
Remote signal/frame/input routes read and write Postgres instead of in-process memory.
Admin frame poll: single in-flight request, chained timing, error backoff.
Stop admin /frame polling during native WebRTC screen share.
Clear relay data from Postgres when remote session ends.
